title: Kadua nukuhivensis
taxonomy: Kadua nukuhivensis is a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family. It belongs to the genus Kadua and is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. The plant is native to the Hawaiian Islands and is also known as the Hawaiian Kadua. It is a type of shrub or vine that produces small white flowers and is often found in coastal and mountainous areas. Kadua nukuhivensis is classified as a species within the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Kadua nukuhivensis has been utilized in traditional Polynesian medicine for various purposes. Its leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis.
